titleStmt.title: SII 28.206: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 206. (A.R. No. 361 of 1913.) CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Naṭarāja temple, west gōpuram, right of entrance.

This record contains three Tamil verses in praise of a Sundara-pāṇḍya, who covered (the temple) with gold (poṉ-mēyṉda). It refers to his having vanquished the Teluṅgas and massacred the northerners (āriyar) who followed them, their dead bodies filling the gound upto Pēṟāṟu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrīḥ—piṟaiyu maravvum yiṟaṅku caṭai mukkaṇiṟai
vaṉiṉṟāṭa viruntāṉiṟai kaṭaṟ pār celiṭṭa teṉṉavaṉicca
m poṟṟirumaṉṟil meliṭṭa poṟṟulaiyil mel §
Ūrampalamāṉa teṉpetai kātalulaki maṉṉar perampa
laiṅkaḷilRead perampalaiyaṅkaḷilkāṉakantaṉṉiṟ pilattil taṅkaḷ tāram pala ko
ṇṭu cārumpaṭi veṉṟu tāraṇiyiṟRead taraṇiyiṟcirampalatte taram
palam poṉmeynta teṉṉavaṉeṅkaḷeṉa vantu māṉaṉ ci
ṟaiyiṭṭirāttiraṇṭu muṭima vantiṟañciya cuntarapāṇ
ṭiyaṉ vāḷilaṉṟu muṉvantu paṭṭa teluṅkar muṇakākka
mutukuramāyp piṉ vanta vāriyār perāṟṟaḷavum piṇam §
Digital edition of SII 28.206 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
